facts.claim: $100,000 grants are paired with an additional $25,000 in Tinker credits.
facts.verbatim_excerpt: We are awarding multiple grants of $100,000 (with an additional $25,000 in Tinker credits) for proposals on research that advances interactivity.
facts.proposal_usage: Budget and feasibility framing: cite cash grant and Tinker credits exactly, without inventing other support.

facts.claim: The program explicitly welcomes evaluations for real-time multimodal interaction, real-time multimodal safety, generative UI explanation, and steering agents during long-horizon tasks.
facts.verbatim_excerpt: Evaluations for real-time multimodal interaction; Ensuring safety for multimodal real-time AI systems; Explaining complex information with generative UI; Steering agents during long-horizon tasks.
facts.proposal_usage: Relevance framing: tie Parker to effortful-speech repair evals, safety gates, caregiver-visible UI, and live steering.

title: Interactivity Research Grants: How to Apply

facts.claim: The application requires a 1–3 page project summary, 1 page budget, PI/contributor list with CVs, location/org/tax/admin details if applicable, and a primary contact email.
facts.verbatim_excerpt: Your proposal must include, at minimum: A 1–3 page summary ... A 1 page budget ... A list of the principal investigator(s) (PIs) and expected contributors with their CVs attached. Location and organizational details including tax ID and admin contact information if applicable. Primary contact email address.
facts.proposal_usage: Submission checklist: keep admin/contact/CV fields human-filled; do not infer private details.

facts.claim: The submission deadline is June 19, 2026 at 11:59 PM PDT.
facts.verbatim_excerpt: Deadline for submission: June 19, 2026 at 11:59 PM PDT.
facts.proposal_usage: Timeline urgency: final package must be reviewed and sent by Pras before the deadline.

facts.claim: Proposals are evaluated on relevance, feasibility, construct validity, and simplicity/generality.
facts.verbatim_excerpt: Proposals will be evaluated on the following criteria: Relevance; Feasibility; Construct validity; Simplicity and generality.
facts.proposal_usage: Proposal structure: every section should map to one of the four named criteria.

facts.claim: Accepted proposals receive the $100k within 30 days after acceptance/tax/wire forms and work with Thinking Machines for up to 6 months.
facts.verbatim_excerpt: Accepted proposals will be awarded the full $100k within 30 days after the proposal is accepted and completed tax and wire detail forms are sent. We will work together for up to 6 months providing model access and support along the way.
facts.proposal_usage: Six-month milestone plan and budget-as-research-plan framing.

title: Interactivity Research Grant Program Terms & Conditions

facts.claim: Applications/proposals are not confidential and may become public, so the Parker packet must not include private family, medical, tax, banking, address, or credential details.
facts.verbatim_excerpt: The Company has no obligation to treat your Application or Proposal as confidential, and you agree that your Proposal may become public. By submitting an Application, you waive any claim that your Proposal is confidential.
facts.proposal_usage: Privacy guardrail: keep public-safe/synthetic evidence only; leave private admin fields to Pras.

facts.claim: Published/disclosed work product must be under CC BY 4.0 or another approved open source license.
facts.verbatim_excerpt: Any Work Product that you publish or disclose to third parties must be made available under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License or such other open source license approved by us.
facts.proposal_usage: Open-source output plan: benchmark cards, synthetic fixtures, scorer, and reports should be license-ready after approval.

facts.claim: Thinking Machines says it claims no ownership rights in participant work product, while retaining broad license rights under the terms.
facts.verbatim_excerpt: We claim no ownership rights in and to any research papers, datasets or other materials developed by a Participant in connection with the Program ("Work Product").
facts.proposal_usage: Terms review note: Pras should review publication/licensing implications before submission or public release.

title: Interaction Models: A Scalable Approach to Human-AI Collaboration

facts.claim: Thinking Machines frames interactivity as something that should scale alongside intelligence, not as an afterthought.
facts.verbatim_excerpt: We think interactivity should scale alongside intelligence; the way we work with AI should not be treated as an afterthought.
facts.proposal_usage: Core thesis alignment: Parker measures collaboration under effortful-speech uncertainty rather than only autonomous task completion.

facts.claim: Interaction models are described as continuous audio/video/text systems with time-aligned micro-turns, which Parker treats as a grant-funded realtime research gap rather than current proof.
facts.verbatim_excerpt: The proposed solution is an interaction model: a model trained from scratch to continuously process audio, video, and text ... Time-aware interaction models see a continuous stream of micro-turns.
facts.proposal_usage: Construct-validity caveat: current Parker evidence is transcript/local; realtime audio/video micro-turn evidence is future funded work.
